description:
## Background The LLM generates decklists that violate Magic's fundamental deck construction rules. Example from a real user session: ``` 4x Sunspine Lynx ← main deck ... 4x Sunspine Lynx ← sideboard ``` 8 copies of the same card across the 75. The system prompt says "do not invent cards" but this is also violated — multiple cards in that session were fictional (Burnout Bashtronaut, Hexing Squelcher, Nova Hellkite, Zhao the Moon Slayer, Soulstone Sanctuary). This bead addresses both with a two-layer approach: prompt hardening and a post-generation validator. --- ## Layer 1 — System Prompt Hardening (assemble.ts) Add a `## Deck Construction Rules` section to `RESPONSE_SYSTEM_BASE` in `src/query/assemble.ts`: ``` ## Deck Construction Rules When generating any decklist, you MUST follow these rules. Violations are illegal and will confuse users. COPY LIMIT: A maximum of 4 copies of any card may appear across the combined mainboard + sideboard. Count copies from BOTH zones together before finalizing. Exception: basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, Forest, and their Snow-Covered and full-art variants) have no copy limit. CORRECT: Main: 4x Shock Side: 2x Shock ← total 6... ILLEGAL Main: 4x Shock Side: 0x Shock ← total 4, legal DECK SIZE: Standard — minimum 60 main, maximum 15 sideboard. Modern — same. CARD EXISTENCE: Only generate real Magic cards that exist in print. If you are not certain a card exists and is legal in the format, do not include it. It is better to use a placeholder comment like "// [add your preferred removal here]" than to invent a card name. FORMAT LEGALITY: Only include cards legal in the format the user is asking about. Do not include banned cards. Before outputting any decklist, mentally count total copies of each non-basic card across main + side. If any card exceeds 4, reduce the sideboard count first. ``` This won't eliminate violations entirely but will catch the most egregious cases where the model loses track of running totals. --- ## Layer 2 — Post-Generation Validator (src/query/decklist.ts) New module with three exported functions. No external dependencies. ### `parseDecklist(text: string): { main: CardEntry[]; side: CardEntry[] } | null` - Searches the LLM response for a markdown code block (` ``` ` fenced) containing decklist content - Parses lines matching: `4x Card Name`, `4 Card Name`, `4x Card Name // comment` - Recognizes section breaks: lines containing "sideboard", "side board", "sb:", "// sideboard" (case-insensitive) switch the parser to sideboard mode - Strips section headers (lines starting with `//` that don't have card counts) - Returns `null` if no parseable decklist is found in the text ```ts type CardEntry = { name: string; qty: number } ``` ### `validateDecklist(main: CardEntry[], side: CardEntry[]): ValidationError[]` - Merges counts across main + side per card name (case-insensitive) - Flags any non-basic-land card with total count > 4 - Flags main deck total < 60 or > 250 (sanity bounds) - Flags sideboard total > 15 ```ts type ValidationError = | { type: 'copy_limit'; card: string; main_qty: number; side_qty: number; total: number } | { type: 'deck_size'; main_total: number } | { type: 'sideboard_size'; side_total: number } ``` Basic land detection: `['plains', 'island', 'swamp', 'mountain', 'forest', 'snow-covered plains', 'snow-covered island', 'snow-covered swamp', 'snow-covered mountain', 'snow-covered forest', 'wastes']` — if name matches (case-insensitive), skip the copy limit check. ### `formatValidationWarning(errors: ValidationError[]): string` - Returns a human-readable string for each error, formatted for display - Example: `"⚠ Decklist issue: 8 copies of Sunspine Lynx found across main and sideboard (max 4). Review before importing to MTGA."` - If multiple errors, bullet-list them --- ## Layer 3 — Wire into the Streaming API Route Location: `src/app/api/query/route.ts` The streaming handler collects delta chunks into a buffer. After the `done` event is processed (full text assembled): 1. Call `parseDecklist(fullText)` 2. If a decklist is found, call `validateDecklist(main, side)` 3. If errors exist, emit a new SSE event before the `done` event: ``` event: decklist_warning data: { "errors": [ { "type": "copy_limit", "card": "Sunspine Lynx", "main_qty": 4, "side_qty": 4, "total": 8 } ], "message": "⚠ Decklist issue: 8 copies of Sunspine Lynx across main and sideboard (max 4)." } ``` This means the frontend receives the warning alongside the complete response, not blocking the stream. --- ## Layer 4 — Frontend Warning Display (src/app/chat/page.tsx) The SSE handler already switches on `currentEvent`. Add a case for `decklist_warning`: - Store the warning message in the oracle message's `meta` object: `meta.decklist_warning: string | null` - After the prose response body, if `msg.meta?.decklist_warning` is set, render: ```tsx
⚠ {msg.meta.decklist_warning}
``` Use `text-flame` (orange-red) — warning, not error. Don't block the response. --- ## What This Does NOT Fix **Hallucinated cards** (e.g., "Burnout Bashtronaut") are a separate problem. Detecting them requires a Scryfall name lookup against the cards table. That's tracked separately — this bead focuses purely on structural rule violations detectable without external DB calls. The copy-limit validator is purely string/count based and runs in O(n) with no DB dependency, making it safe to run on every response. --- ## Files to Touch - `src/query/assemble.ts` — add deck construction rules to RESPONSE_SYSTEM_BASE - `src/query/decklist.ts` — new file: parseDecklist, validateDecklist, formatValidationWarning - `src/app/api/query/route.ts` — wire validator into stream handler, emit decklist_warning event - `src/app/chat/page.tsx` — handle decklist_warning SSE event, render warning in message meta footer --- ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] System prompt includes deck construction rules section with copy limit, basic land exception, deck size - [ ] `parseDecklist` correctly extracts main + side from markdown code blocks in LLM output - [ ] `validateDecklist` detects: copy limit violations (non-basic only), sideboard > 15, main < 60 - [ ] Basic lands (Mountain, Forest, etc.) are exempt from copy limit check - [ ] Copy limit violation in the reproducer case (4x Sunspine Lynx main + 4x side) produces a `copy_limit` error - [ ] `decklist_warning` SSE event is emitted when violations are found - [ ] Warning renders in the chat UI below the response, in flame color - [ ] Responses without decklists are unaffected (no false positives) - [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes - [ ] Unit tests for parseDecklist and validateDecklist covering: valid deck (no warning), copy limit violation, basic land exemption, sideboard overflow, no decklist in response
title: Enforce decklist correctness — 4-copy rule validator and prompt hardening

description:
Navigating directly to /data/modern/[archetype-id] (e.g. /data/modern/mono-black-tron-modern) shows empty content — all Suspense sections render their empty states. Navigating client-side (homepage → /data/modern → click archetype in MetaShareBar) loads the page correctly.

ROOT CAUSE (high confidence):
src/app/data/[format]/[archetype]/queries.ts uses createStaticClient() for all data fetching — including the runtime Server Component queries (ArchetypeHeader, ShareOverTimeSection, RecentResultsSection, WinRateOverTimeSection). The comment on createStaticClient explicitly says it is for 'build-time / static generation contexts (generateStaticParams, generateMetadata) where cookies() is not available.'

Client-side navigation works because it serves the pre-rendered RSC payload from generateStaticParams (which ran createStaticClient at build time — the correct context). Direct navigation triggers a fresh server render. In that context, createStaticClient() uses bare @supabase/supabase-js with no explicit fetch cache config, which may interact badly with Next.js App Router's fetch deduplication/caching and return empty results.

The format overview page (src/app/data/[format]/queries.ts) uses createClient() from @supabase/ssr throughout and does not have this issue.

FIX:
In src/app/data/[format]/[archetype]/queries.ts:
- Keep createStaticClient() only in fetchAllArchetypeIds() (used by generateStaticParams) and fetchArchetype() when called from generateMetadata
- Switch all other query functions (fetchLatestSnapshot, fetchShareHistory, fetchHasMatches, fetchRecentResults, and fetchArchetype when called at runtime) to use createClient() from @/lib/supabase-server

The cleanest approach: change all query functions to accept a supabase client as a parameter, pass createStaticClient() from generateStaticParams/generateMetadata and createClient() from runtime Server Components. Or simply switch all runtime queries to createClient() — cookies are available in all Server Component render contexts.

ALSO INVESTIGATE:
If the fix above doesn't resolve it, check whether the archetype_id in the URL matches the archetype_id in metagame_snapshots. A mismatch (e.g. archetypes table has 'mono-black-tron-modern' but snapshots reference a different id) would cause the archetype header to render (fetchArchetype finds a row) but all data sections to be empty (no matching snapshots/deck_archetypes rows).

REPRO STEPS:
1. Navigate directly to /data/modern/mono-black-tron-modern (or any archetype slug)
2. Observe: all sections show empty states
3. Navigate to / → /data/modern → click the same archetype in the MetaShareBar
4. Observe: page loads correctly with full data

ACCEPTANCE:
Direct navigation and client-side navigation to the same archetype URL produce identical content.
title: Bug: archetype detail page empty on direct navigation, works via client-side nav
Phase 2 audit — test query pipeline end-to-end with real data
MTGA/MTGO deck list output formatting

Track as known limitation; fix in Phase 2 alongside archetype classifier.

description: intent.ts line 41: return parsed as Intent with only a typeof object check. If the LLM returns timeframe_days: undefined or an invalid value, 86_400_000 * undefined = NaN, producing an Invalid Date cutoff that breaks all range queries silently. Fix: normalize timeframe_days to one of [30,60,90] defaulting to 90, normalize format to one of ['modern','standard'] or null.
title: intent: validate LLM output before casting — bad timeframe_days causes NaN date cutoff, missing format silently returns empty results
Archetype clustering pipeline — Jaccard + LLM labeling + card signature

description:
The empty state structure is correct (centered, query counter present) but the visual energy is too low. Three targeted fixes:

1. Ambient glow opacity: in chat/page.tsx the radial gradient is rgba(79,142,247,0.04) — 4% is invisible. Increase to rgba(79,142,247,0.07) so it registers subtly without being heavy.

2. Prompt chip text brightness: the Button secondary variant uses text-ash by default, making chips look like muted gray boxes. Add text-ink/80 to the chip className so text reads as bright at rest, not just on hover. Current: className='justify-start text-left bg-surface hover:bg-edge px-4 py-3.5 hover:glow-spark-sm'. Add text-ink/80 to that.

3. Subtitle opacity: 'Ask about the current metagame...' is text-ink/60. Bump to text-ink/75 — slightly more presence without competing with the chips.

4. Verify hover:glow-spark-sm is rendering correctly on chip hover in the browser (Tailwind JIT custom class with variant). If not, replace with an explicit hover:box-shadow via a wrapper or inline style.

5. Note for engineering: the '5 queries available today' counter is hardcoded. Wire it to actual remaining query count from the user session once auth + rate limiting (mindsculptor-toy.7) is complete.
title: Design: empty state polish — brightness and energy

description:
The /chat empty state is a massive black void with prompt chips floating in the middle. It looks abandoned. Fix:

1. Center content vertically in the available space between nav and input bar (use flexbox column with justify-content: center). The prompt chips should be in the visual middle of the screen, not the top third.
2. Add a subtle ambient glow behind the prompt area — a radial gradient from rgba(79,142,247,0.04) to transparent, centered on the chip cluster. Creates a faint 'portal' effect without being heavy.
3. The subtitle 'Ask about the current metagame...' is fine but increase opacity. Muted text at this size almost disappears.
4. Prompt chips: 2x2 grid layout is good. Increase chip padding (more breathing room), add surface background (#0C1220), visible border (#172035), and hover state (border shifts to #4F8EF7 with faint glow). They should look like something you'd want to click.
5. Add a small query counter above the input bar in the empty state: '5 queries available today' (Casual) — makes the product feel alive even before the user types.

Acceptance: the empty state feels intentional and inviting, not like an empty page waiting to load.
title: Design: oracle empty state redesign

description:
The History button exists in the Nav (opens a Drawer) and is stubbed with "Coming in toy.8". The tiers.md spec references history gating (Casual: 7-day lookback, Spike: unlimited) but chat history has no implementation plan yet. Before speccing the engineering work, answer these product questions:

1. **What is a "history" unit?** A full conversation thread (multi-turn), or individual oracle responses? The current chat is a single continuous thread per session — do sessions need explicit names/titles, or are they just timestamped?

2. **Where does history live?** The existing History drawer in the Nav (good for quick access, constrained space) or a dedicated `/chat/history` page (better for browsing many sessions), or both?

3. **Persistence model.** Messages are currently held in React state and lost on refresh. A `CHAT_STORAGE_KEY` localStorage hack exists only for OAuth redirect preservation. Real history needs server-side storage. Options: (a) `chat_sessions` + `chat_messages` tables in Supabase, (b) single `chat_sessions` table with messages as a JSONB column (simpler, not queryable). Which direction?

4. **Gating.** tiers.md says Casual = 7-day lookback, Spike = unlimited. Does anonymous get any history (probably not — no identity to attach it to)? Does history save passively (every query auto-saved) or does the user explicitly save a conversation?

5. **Resume behavior.** Can a user click a past conversation and continue it? Or is history read-only (view old responses but always start fresh)? If resumable, does it count against their daily query limit?

6. **Session boundaries.** What ends a session? Tab close? 30 minutes of inactivity? Explicit "New chat" button? This affects how threads are grouped in the history view.

Once these are answered, the engineering work likely splits into: (a) DB schema + persistence layer, (b) history drawer/page UI, (c) tier-gated access logic.
title: Chat history — product decisions needed before speccing

description:
## Arcana Theme — "Spell Tome"

A fourth theme for users who want full immersion. High-fantasy aesthetic: weathered parchment, flowing arcane typography, selective purple glow effects.

### Visual Design

**Color palette:**
- `--color-canvas`: `244 232 201` (parchment #F4E8C9)
- `--color-surface`: `235 220 185` (aged parchment #EBDCB9)
- `--color-edge`: `180 155 110` (worn leather border)
- `--color-spark`: `124 58 237` (arcane purple #7C3AED)
- `--color-flame`: `220 38 38` (blood red)
- `--color-gold`: `161 98 7` (burnished gold)
- `--color-copper`: `146 64 14` (aged copper)
- `--color-ink`: `28 20 12` (dark brown ink)
- `--color-ash`: `101 75 46` (faded ink)

**Background texture:**
- Replace blueprint grid with CSS radial-gradient age-spot pattern
- Subtle vignette at edges via box-shadow inset on body or radial-gradient overlay
- No grid lines — organic, worn feel

### Typography

**New CSS variables (added in mindsculptor-usx):**
- `--font-display`: controls heading font family
- `--font-body`: controls body/prose font family
- `--text-glow`: CSS drop-shadow or text-shadow value for selective glow effects

**Arcana values:**
- `--font-display`: `'Cinzel', 'Palatino Linotype', Georgia, serif`
- `--font-body`: `'IM Fell English', 'Garamond', 'Palatino Linotype', serif`
- `--text-glow`: `0 0 8px rgb(124 58 237 / 0.6), 0 0 16px rgb(124 58 237 / 0.3)`

**Google Fonts to load (via next/font or <link> in layout):**
- Cinzel (weights 400, 700)
- IM Fell English (weight 400, italic variant)

**Usage:** Apply `--font-display` to h1/h2/h3 via Tailwind class (e.g., `font-display`), `--font-body` to prose content. Other themes use system defaults (--font-display: inherit, --font-body: inherit).

**Selective glow:** Apply `text-glow` utility class (defined in globals.css) only to: spark-colored numbers (meta share %, tier badges), trend arrows, key stat values. NOT applied globally — too heavy-handed.

### Implementation Scope

1. Add `[data-theme="arcana"]` block to globals.css with all CSS variables
2. Wire up background texture (radial-gradient replacing blueprint grid — conditional on theme)
3. Load Cinzel + IM Fell English via next/font — conditional import or via CSS @import scoped to arcana
4. Add `font-display` and `font-body` Tailwind utilities using `--font-display` and `--font-body` variables
5. Add `text-glow` utility class using `--text-glow` variable
6. Apply `font-display` to headings (h1-h3), `font-body` to `.prose-oracle` content when theme is arcana
7. Apply `text-glow` selectively to spark-colored stat values

### Dependencies
- mindsculptor-usx must include `--font-display`, `--font-body`, `--text-glow` variables (all themes define them; non-arcana themes set them to `inherit` / `none`)
- mindsculptor-5ae must be complete (theme switcher UI exists, `data-theme` mechanism works)

### Out of Scope
- No animated effects (candle flicker, particle effects) — future P4 bead if ever
- No custom icons or SVG ornaments in this phase
title: Arcana theme — spell tome aesthetic (Cinzel + parchment + arcane glow)
SQL retrieval layer — query tournaments/decks based on intent

description:
Several components are using off-spec colors. Fix the following:

1. Trend arrows (↑ ↓ → on landing and metagame pages): currently using generic green (#22c55e) and red. Use design system colors: upward trend = #4F8EF7 (electric blue), downward trend = #D4552A (flame-red), neutral → = #4A5878 (muted). The green reads as generic and clashes with the Izzet palette.
2. User message bubbles in /chat: currently unstyled plain bubble. Give them a surface background (#172035), slightly rounded corners, and right-aligned with padding. Should feel like a distinct authored message, not a plain text label.
3. 'See metagame charts →' ghost button on landing: barely visible. Increase border opacity to #4A5878 at rest, text to #E4EEFF. On hover, border shifts to #4F8EF7. Needs to be a real secondary action, not an afterthought.
4. MEDIUM / HIGH / LOW confidence badge: the gold color (#C9A050) is correct for MEDIUM — keep it. Verify LOW uses #D4552A (flame-red) and HIGH uses #4F8EF7 (electric blue) for a clear signal hierarchy.
5. The 'Full breakdown →' link color in the metagame section (landing): currently electric blue — that's correct, keep it.

Acceptance: no green in the UI anywhere. Trend indicators, user bubbles, and confidence badges all use the design system palette.
title: Design: component color corrections

description:
Theme switcher UI and theme definitions. Allows users to switch between Izzet Dark (default), Blueprint (light), and Terminal (green-on-black). Persists to localStorage. Requires mindsculptor-usx (CSS variable refactor) to ship first.

---

## THREE THEMES

### Izzet Dark (default)
The current design. Deep navy-black backgrounds, electric blue accent, Izzet flame-red secondary.
Theme key: 'dark'

### Blueprint (light)
Blueprint paper aesthetic — maintains the schematic/analytical vibe in light mode.
Cool light blue-gray background (#EFF2F8), not pure white. Preserves the 'technical document' feeling.
Deep navy text. Deeper blue accent (#2563EB) for contrast on white — same family as spark, still Izzet.
Theme key: 'light'

### Terminal (dark, green-on-black)
Retro terminal aesthetic. Appeals to the MTGO-playing grinder demographic.
Near-black with green tint background. Terminal green (#22C55E) replaces electric blue as primary accent.
Amber (#F97316) replaces flame-red. Light green-white text.
Theme key: 'terminal'

(The color values are defined in mindsculptor-usx. This bead owns the switcher UI only.)

---

## THEME HOOK

Create src/hooks/useTheme.ts:

  const THEMES = ['dark', 'light', 'terminal'] as const
  type Theme = typeof THEMES[number]

  export function useTheme() {
    const [theme, setThemeState] = useState<Theme>('dark')

    useEffect(() => {
      // Read from DOM (set by FOBT inline script) — source of truth
      const t = document.documentElement.getAttribute('data-theme') as Theme || 'dark'
      setThemeState(t)
    }, [])

    function setTheme(t: Theme) {
      document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', t)
      localStorage.setItem('fm_theme', t)
      setThemeState(t)
    }

    return { theme, setTheme, themes: THEMES }
  }

Note: initial state is 'dark' to match SSR — the useEffect syncs to the actual value from localStorage/FOBT.
This avoids hydration mismatches.

---

## THEME SWITCHER COMPONENT

Create src/components/ThemeSwitcher.tsx

A small icon button in the nav that opens a dropdown popover with theme options.

### Trigger button
- Icon: a circle split into dark/light halves (☯ feel, but custom SVG) — or use a simple palette icon
- 16x16px, text-ash, hover:text-ink
- aria-label="Switch theme"
- Positioned in the nav between the nav links and the auth section (see Nav placement below)

### Dropdown popover
- Opens below the trigger on click, closes on outside click or Escape
- Width: ~180px
- Three rows, one per theme:

  ┌──────────────────────────────┐
  │ ◉ Izzet Dark       ●●●●●    │  ← active checkmark + color swatches
  │   Blueprint        ○○○○○    │
  │   Terminal         ●●●●●    │
  └──────────────────────────────┘

### Color swatches (per row)
5 small circles (8px each, 4px gap) showing the palette:
  canvas → surface → edge → spark → ink
These give an instant visual preview without needing labels.

### Row styling
- Active theme: text-ink, checkmark icon (✓) left of name
- Inactive: text-ash, no checkmark, hover:text-ink hover:bg-surface
- Swatch circles: inline-flex, rounded-full, border border-edge/30

### Swatch colors
Since CSS variables are active, the swatch circles for the current theme naturally show the right colors.
For inactive themes, hardcode the swatch colors as inline styles (they don't use Tailwind tokens):

  Izzet Dark:  ['#06080F','#0C1220','#172035','#4F8EF7','#E4EEFF']
  Blueprint:   ['#EFF2F8','#FFFFFF','#CDD4E8','#2563EB','#0F172A']
  Terminal:    ['#0A0F0A','#0F160F','#1A2E1A','#22C55E','#DCFCE7']

---

## NAV PLACEMENT

Update src/components/Nav.tsx. Add ThemeSwitcher between the nav links section and the auth section:

  BEFORE:
  [⚡ Firemind] [Chat][Data]                    [Sign in] [Go Spike ↑]

  AFTER:
  [⚡ Firemind] [Chat][Data]         [◑] [Sign in] [Go Spike ↑]

  The ◑ icon is the ThemeSwitcher trigger. It sits between the left nav and the right auth cluster.
  Use ml-auto on the ThemeSwitcher's wrapper to push it right, then the auth buttons follow naturally.

Authenticated nav:
  [⚡ Firemind] [Chat][Data]    [◑] [🕐][🔔][avatar▾]

---

## SETTINGS PAGE

Also expose the theme switcher in /settings (when that page exists) as a labeled setting row:

  Appearance
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ Theme       [Izzet Dark ▾] (dropdown)       │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘

This gives a more explicit, labeled control for users who don't notice the nav icon.
For now (no /settings page yet): nav-only is sufficient.

---

## PERSISTENCE & SSR BEHAVIOR

- Theme stored in localStorage key 'fm_theme'
- FOBT inline script (from mindsculptor-usx) ensures correct theme on initial load — no flash
- useTheme hook reads from DOM attribute (set by FOBT script) on mount to sync React state
- SSR always renders with dark theme (matches :root defaults in CSS) — the inline script corrects
  any non-dark preference before first paint

---

## ADDING FUTURE THEMES

To add a 4th theme:
  1. Add CSS variables for [data-theme="newtheme"] in globals.css
  2. Add 'newtheme' to the THEMES array in useTheme.ts
  3. Add swatch colors to ThemeSwitcher.tsx
That's it — no other changes needed.

---

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] useTheme hook reads from DOM on mount, no hydration mismatch
- [ ] setTheme updates data-theme attribute, localStorage, and React state atomically
- [ ] ThemeSwitcher renders in nav, trigger is accessible (aria-label)
- [ ] Dropdown shows all 3 themes with correct swatches and active checkmark
- [ ] Clicking a theme applies it instantly (no reload)
- [ ] Selected theme persists across page navigation and browser refresh
- [ ] Blueprint theme: all text is readable on light backgrounds (no white-on-white)
- [ ] Terminal theme: all text is readable on dark green backgrounds
- [ ] Opacity modifiers still work (bg-canvas/80 etc.) in all three themes
- [ ] npx tsc --noEmit passes
title: Theme switcher — Izzet Dark, Blueprint, Terminal

description:
The page background and content surfaces are nearly identical darkness, making the UI feel flat and gloomy. Fix:

1. Background should be #06080F (deep navy-black). Content surfaces (cards, panels, metagame section, oracle response area, input container, nav bar) should be #0C1220 — visibly distinct from background.
2. Nav bar: add a bottom border at #172035 so it separates from page content. Currently bleeds into the page.
3. Metagame section on landing page: wrap in a surface-colored container (#0C1220) with a subtle border (#172035). Right now it floats on the same black as the background with no visual container.
4. Primary body text should be #E4EEFF (cool blue-white). Currently rendering as muted gray. Check that text-primary token is applied consistently.
5. Hero section: 'Before you register.' subtitle is too visually quiet — same weight as secondary text. Both headline lines should feel bold and confident. Consider making the subtitle slightly smaller but full-opacity, not muted.
6. Input bar at the bottom of /chat: wrap in a surface container (#0C1220) with a top border (#172035) to separate it from the page. Currently it floats without grounding.

Acceptance: screenshot shows clear visual depth between background, surface, and elevated elements. Text is crisp and bright, not gray.
title: Design: surface differentiation + text contrast

description: We already parse MTGO Challenge and Preliminary standings with W-L records. Aggregate these per archetype to compute approximate match win rates. Biased toward top performers but still useful signal. Depends on archetype classification existing.
title: Aggregate archetype win rates from MTGO Challenge/Prelim W-L records

description:
Deck list generation from the chat feature always produces empty sideboards. Root cause is likely upstream: MTGO's decklists page does not publish sideboard data, so deck_cards has 0 sideboard entries in the DB (confirmed — this has been a known limitation since Phase 1).

Investigation required:
1. Confirm deck_cards sideboard count is actually 0 in prod: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM deck_cards WHERE is_sideboard = true (or equivalent)
2. Check whether MTGGoldfish scraper (mindsculptor-aws) or MTGTop8 scraper (mindsculptor-397) ingest sideboard data — these may be viable sources
3. If sideboard data exists from non-MTGO sources, verify the retrieval layer (src/query/retrieval.ts) is not filtering it out
4. If no sideboard data exists in DB at all, the fix is in the ingestion pipeline: identify which scrape source has sideboards and parse them into deck_cards with the sideboard flag set

Acceptance: generated deck lists include a valid 15-card sideboard drawn from real tournament data.
title: Bug: deck list exports have empty sideboards

description: attachDeckCosts computes minCoverage = totalQty * 0.75. A deck with 24 basics (no price) + 36 spells (priced) hits 60% coverage and returns null cost correctly. But if 40 cheap cantrips (priced) + 20 expensive lands (not priced) → 67% coverage, null returned correctly. The problem is inverse: 40 priced spells + 4 fetch lands (unpriced, high cost) = 91% coverage threshold passed but cost is missing 60-80% of actual value. Fix: null out cost when any card slot with estimated value >  is unpriced, or more practically, check coverage against the number of *unique card names* not raw quantity.
title: retrieval: deck cost coverage check is quantity-based not value-based — 75% of card copies having prices can still miss most of the cost

description: mtgtop8.ts line 83 and mtggoldfish equivalent: if no date found in HTML, defaults to new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]. This silently attributes an old tournament to today, inflating recency scores in snapshots and retrieval. Should skip the tournament or set date to null (if column allows) rather than today.
title: parsers: date fallback should be null not today — undated tournaments silently land in today's recency window

description:
## Root Cause

In `src/app/chat/page.tsx`:

```ts
const [remaining, setRemaining] = useState<number | null>(null)
...
const used = isAnon ? anonCount : (remaining != null ? limit - remaining : 0)
const displayRemaining = limit - used
```

`remaining` initializes to `null`. Before any query is made, `used` falls through to `0`, so `displayRemaining = USER_LIMIT - 0 = 10`. The true count is only fetched as a side-effect of making a query (via the `rate_limit` field in the SSE `meta` event). Hard refresh drops that state.

## Fix

Add a `useEffect` that fetches the user's current daily usage from `/api/query-status` (or inline via Supabase browser client) on mount, conditional on `user` being non-null.

### Option A — dedicated API route (preferred)

New route: `GET /api/query-status`
- Reads auth from cookies (same as the existing query route)
- Queries `oracle_queries` table for today's count for this user
- Returns `{ remaining: number, resets_at: string }`

In `ChatPageInner`, after auth state resolves:
```ts
useEffect(() => {
  if (!user) return
  fetch('/api/query-status')
    .then(r => r.json())
    .then(d => {
      setRemaining(d.remaining)
      setResetsAt(d.resets_at)
    })
    .catch(() => {}) // best effort, don't block UI
}, [user])
```

### Option B — Supabase browser client query (simpler, no new route)

Query `oracle_queries` directly from the browser client on mount. Avoids the extra route but couples the client to the DB schema.

Option A is preferred — keeps DB logic server-side, consistent with the rest of the query pipeline.

## Notes

- The fetch should only run when `user` is set. Anon counter already uses localStorage (no DB), so this only affects logged-in users.
- Show a loading skeleton or leave the counter blank (`—`) until the fetch resolves, rather than showing `10` as a false default. This avoids the jarring flash.
- If the fetch fails, default to `USER_LIMIT` (optimistic) and don't block the UI.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Hard refresh while logged in shows accurate remaining count within ~500ms, not `10`
- [ ] Counter blank or skeleton while fetch is in-flight (no false `10`)
- [ ] Anon behavior unchanged
- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes
title: Hard refresh shows stale '10 queries left' instead of real count

description:
## Context

Mobile is a first-class experience. This bead is a comprehensive audit and fix pass — not a quick tweak. Every page and component must be reviewed at 375px (iPhone SE) and 390px (iPhone 14). The outcome must be a UI that feels intentional at all viewport sizes, not just "doesn't break."

---

## Known Issues (spotted in code review)

### 1. Nav — critical overflow
`Nav.tsx`: a single `flex` row with logo + two NavLinks + icon buttons + "Go Spike ↑" button in `px-6 h-12`. At 375px this overflows immediately. "Metagame Data" alone is ~110px, the full row is 450-500px in a 375px container.

**Fix: mobile nav collapse**
- Breakpoint: `md:` (768px) shows the current desktop layout
- Below `md`: show logo + hamburger icon on the right; hide NavLinks, icon buttons, Go Spike
- Hamburger opens a full-width slide-down or overlay menu with: Chat, Metagame Data, separator, Sign in / account, Go Spike
- Keep the sticky bar + backdrop blur — just collapse the items

### 2. MetaShareBar recharts — broken on mobile
`MetaShareBar.tsx`: `margin={{ left: 160, right: 20 }}` is fixed. At 375px − 48px padding = 327px content → 327 − 160 − 20 = **147px for bars**. That's survivable but the `CustomYTick` `foreignObject` is `width={200} x={-200}`, meaning the archetype name labels render outside the SVG bounds and clip.

**Fix: responsive left margin**
- Use a `useWindowWidth` or measure container width with `useRef` + `ResizeObserver`
- On `< 640px`: collapse to showing only top 8 archetypes (not 15), reduce left margin to 110px, shorten archetype names to first 14 chars + ellipsis
- Alternative: swap the chart to a vertical list layout (plain div rows with a CSS bar) below `sm:` — avoids recharts SVG complexity entirely on mobile and is more touch-friendly

The vertical list approach is preferred: renders identical data as CSS `<div style={{ width: `${pct}%` }}>` bars, links intact, no foreignObject clipping. Recharts chart remains for `md:` and up.

### 3. RecentResults table — horizontal scroll is poor UX
6-column table with `overflow-x-auto`. On mobile users don't discover horizontal scroll naturally, and the table is too dense.

**Fix: responsive table / card layout**
- Below `sm:`: render results as stacked cards instead of table rows
- Each card: placement (large, colored) + event name (linked) + date + tier badge + pilot + record in a compact 2-column grid
- `sm:` and up: current table layout

### 4. Chat page — suggested prompts grid
`grid grid-cols-2 gap-3` is always 2 columns. At 375px each button is ~150px wide. Prompts like "What should I play at my RCQ this weekend?" will truncate.

**Fix:** `grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 gap-3` — stack on mobile, 2-col from sm up.

### 5. Chat input bar — padding and button size
`px-6 py-4` with `flex gap-3` Input + Button. At 375px: 375 − 48px padding = 327px. Input + "Ask" button in 327px is manageable but tight. Button should be `shrink-0` (already is in OracleInput but check chat page). The `py-4` padding on the sticky input bar consumes significant vertical space on mobile — consider `py-3` on mobile.

### 6. Landing page CTAs
`flex items-center gap-4` with two buttons side by side. At 375px: "Ask the Firemind — it's free" button + "See metagame charts →" will either overflow or wrap awkwardly.

**Fix:** `flex flex-col sm:flex-row items-start sm:items-center gap-3`

### 7. Archetype detail — header stat row
`flex items-baseline gap-4 flex-wrap` — wraps fine. But `px-6 py-12` outer padding: `py-12` is 48px top/bottom. On mobile that's a lot. Consider `py-8 md:py-12`.

### 8. Format overview — range selector
Range buttons (`30d / 60d / 90d`) are rendered in a `flex gap-2`. If the format page header stats row is also in a flex row, it may overflow. Check at 375px.

### 9. TrendLines chart (recharts LineChart)
Has `margin={{ top: 5, right: 10, left: -20, bottom: 5 }}`. The negative left margin and small right margin may cause axis labels to clip on narrow screens. Review at 375px.

### 10. Touch targets
Audit every button/link for 44×44px minimum touch target (WCAG 2.5.5). Known small targets:
- NavLink pills: `text-sm px-3 py-1.5` → ~36px tall
- Range selector buttons: same
- "Go Spike ↑" button: `px-3 py-1.5` → ~36px tall
- MetaShareBar archetype name links inside SVG foreignObject — likely untappable

---

## Viewport Test Matrix

Every fix must be verified at:
| Width | Device |
|---|---|
| 375px | iPhone SE / older iPhones |
| 390px | iPhone 14 Pro |
| 414px | iPhone 14 Plus |
| 768px | iPad mini portrait |
| 1024px | iPad landscape / small laptop |
| 1280px+ | Desktop (must not regress) |

---

## Pages to Audit

1. `/` — Landing page
2. `/chat` — Chat (empty state + active state with messages)
3. `/data/modern` — Format overview (MetaShareBar, TopMovers, TrendLines)
4. `/data/modern/[archetype]` — Archetype detail (header stats, RecentResults table, ShareOverTime, Oracle CTAs)
5. Nav (all states: logged out, logged in, active path)

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Nav does not overflow at 375px; hamburger menu opens/closes cleanly
- [ ] MetaShareBar is readable and usable at 375px (either responsive margins or list layout swap)
- [ ] RecentResults table has card layout below `sm:`
- [ ] Chat suggested prompts stack to 1 column below `sm:`
- [ ] Landing CTAs stack vertically on mobile
- [ ] All interactive elements meet 44px minimum touch target height
- [ ] No horizontal page overflow at 375px on any page (test with `overflow-x: hidden` on body, nothing clips weirdly)
- [ ] Charts (ShareOverTime, TrendLines) are legible at 375px — axis labels not clipping, bars not invisible
- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes
- [ ] Visually tested at all widths in the test matrix above
title: Mobile responsiveness audit and fix — comprehensive pass

description:
## Problem

The chat UI has no conversation continuity. Each message is an isolated single-turn LLM call — no history is sent anywhere in the stack.

Concrete failure: user asked a question, Firemind responded with some incorrect card details, user corrected those details in a follow-up message. Firemind agreed but said 'whoever claimed those things was wrong' — because it had zero memory of being the one who said them two messages ago.

## Root cause

The entire pipeline is stateless:
- Chat UI sends `{ query: string }` only — no history
- API route: `handleQuery(body.query)` — single string, no history
- `handleQuery` calls `llm.complete(system, user)` — one-shot completion
- `llm.complete` signature is `(system, user, opts)` — no messages array

## What to build

Thread the last 6 messages (3 user + 3 oracle exchanges) as conversation history through the full stack. Last 6 is chosen to cover immediate correction loops without unbounded token growth.

**Intent extraction stays single-turn** — it runs on only the current query string, not history. Full history in the intent prompt would be noisy and expensive.

### Changes required

1. `src/lib/llm.ts` — add `completeWithHistory(system, history, opts)` where history is `{ role: 'user' | 'assistant', content: string }[]`. Keep existing `complete()` for single-turn uses (intent extraction, etc).

2. `src/query/index.ts` — `handleQuery(query, history?)` accepts optional prior messages. Passes history to the LLM call via `completeWithHistory`. History should NOT be passed to `extractIntent` — intent extraction stays single-turn on the current query.

3. `src/app/api/query/route.ts` — accept optional `messages` array in the request body alongside `query`. Validate shape, pass to `handleQuery`. Truncate to last 6 messages server-side as a safety bound.

4. `src/app/chat/page.tsx` — on each submit, send the current `messages` state (last 6, excluding the message being submitted) as `messages` in the POST body. Map oracle role to 'assistant' for the API.

### History format sent to API
```ts
{
  query: string,
  messages?: { role: 'user' | 'assistant', content: string }[]
}
```

### LLM call shape
The Claude API messages array should be:
  [...history, { role: 'user', content: currentUserMessage }]
where currentUserMessage is the assembled context + query (same as current single-turn user message).

## Acceptance criteria
- Follow-up corrections work: model knows it was the one who made prior claims
- Follow-up questions work: 'which of those is cheapest?' after a deck list response works correctly
- No history → behavior identical to today (backward compatible)
- Intent extraction is NOT affected (still single-turn)
- History capped at 6 messages server-side regardless of what client sends
- Existing tests pass
title: Thread conversation history through the query pipeline

description:
Public SSR page, no auth required. The main SEO surface — one page per archetype per format. Crawlable. revalidate: 3600.

URL: /data/[format]/[archetypeId] where archetypeId is the archetype slug (e.g. 'izzet-murktide-modern')
generateStaticParams: pre-render all known archetypes at build time using SELECT id, format FROM archetypes.
Unknown archetype IDs return 404.

---

DATA QUERIES (parallel, Server Component)

1. Archetype header:
   SELECT id, name, format, tier, description, key_cards
   FROM archetypes WHERE id = [archetypeId]
   → 404 if not found

2. Latest snapshot stats (meta share, win rate, trend):
   SELECT meta_share, trend_delta, top8_count, total_entries, sample_size, confidence, window_end
   FROM metagame_snapshots
   WHERE archetype_id = [archetypeId]
   ORDER BY window_end DESC LIMIT 1

3. Historical snapshots for charts:
   SELECT meta_share, window_end
   FROM metagame_snapshots
   WHERE archetype_id = [archetypeId]
   ORDER BY window_end ASC
   -- Used for ShareOverTime chart

4. Win rate over time (from matches table):
   For each snapshot window, compute:
     wins WHERE (deck_a_id in archetype decks AND winner = player_a)
              OR (deck_b_id in archetype decks AND winner = player_b)
     total = wins + losses (excluding byes and draws)
     win_rate = wins / total
   
   IMPORTANT: matches table is likely empty right now (just created). This query
   should return null gracefully — the win rate section shows a pending state, not an error.
   Check: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM matches — if 0, skip this query entirely and show pending state.

5. Recent results (last 10 top-placing decks for this archetype):
   SELECT d.pilot, d.placement, d.record, t.name, t.date, t.tier, t.source_url
   FROM decks d
   JOIN tournaments t ON t.id = d.tournament_id
   JOIN deck_archetypes da ON da.deck_id = d.id
   WHERE da.archetype_id = [archetypeId]
     AND d.placement IS NOT NULL
   ORDER BY t.date DESC, d.placement ASC
   LIMIT 10

All queries run in parallel. Archetype header is fetched first (for 404 check), rest in parallel.

---

COMPONENTS

ArchetypeHeader:
- Archetype name (h1)
- Format badge (Modern / Standard pill)
- Tier badge (S/A/B/C) — color same as toy.9
- Meta share % (from latest snapshot) — large, prominent
- Trend arrow from trend_delta: ↑ text-spark, ↓ text-flame, → text-ash
- 'Based on [sample_size] decks · [confidence]' — same ConfidenceBadge as toy.9
- Win rate % if available, 'Win rate pending' (text-ash, italic) if matches table empty

ShareOverTime:
- Recharts AreaChart (single series looks better as area than line)
- X axis: window_end dates
- Y axis: meta_share %
- Fill: spark blue at 20% opacity, stroke at full opacity
- Time range toggle: 30d / 90d / all — client-side filter of already-fetched data (no refetch)
- Minimum data: >= 3 points. Empty state: 'Not enough history yet'
- GOTCHA: with only 4 tournaments currently in DB, this chart will likely show 1-2 points.
  Show gracefully: render the points as dots on the area chart, no connecting line if < 3 points.
  Display note: 'More data accumulates with each event — trend improves over time'

WinRateOverTime:
- Recharts LineChart, single series
- Same time range toggle as ShareOverTime (shared state)
- If matches table is empty (detected at data fetch time, passed as prop):
  Show placeholder panel: '⏳ Win rate data will appear as match records are ingested'
  styled like a chart skeleton but with a text message — NOT an error, just pending
- When data exists: win_rate % over time, stroke text-gold (#C9A050)

RecentResults:
- Plain table, no chart library needed
- Columns: Placement | Event | Tier | Pilot | Date | Record (if available)
- Placement: 1st gets text-gold, top 4 gets text-spark, rest text-ash
- Event tier badge: Challenge / Preliminary / RCQ — small pill
- Pilot name is plain text (no link — MTGO pilot pages are ephemeral)
- Source link: external link icon on event name linking to tournaments.source_url
- Empty state: 'No results recorded yet for this archetype'
- This section works with current data — most likely to have real content on first ship

OracleCTAs:
Always shown. Three actions, each clearly labeled with tier requirement:
  1. '⚡ Ask the Firemind about [archetype name]' — free, links to /chat?q=... with pre-filled query
     Pre-filled query example: 'Tell me about Izzet Murktide in Modern — is it worth playing right now?'
  2. 'Generate sample deck list' — Spike only. For now (no gating yet): show as active,
     links to /chat?q=Generate a sample 75 for [archetype] in [format]
     NOTE: when gating is built (toy.3), this becomes a soft gate with upgrade prompt for Casual
  3. 'Sideboard plan vs the field' — same as above, Spike-only post-gating

AlertBell (🔔):
- 'Alert me when this archetype top 8s'
- For now (no auth/gating): clicking opens the auth modal (Sign in to set alerts)
- Post-toy.1: Casual gets 1 alert, Spike gets unlimited

---

LOADING STATES

Each section in its own Suspense boundary:
- ArchetypeHeader skeleton: gray pill badges + two large gray stat blocks
- ShareOverTime skeleton: gray area chart shape (SVG rect with rounded top)
- WinRateOverTime skeleton: gray line chart shape or pending state if no match data
- RecentResults skeleton: 5 gray table rows, alternating opacity

---

EMPTY / ERROR STATES

- Archetype not found: full-page 404 with 'Archetype not found — it may not have been labeled yet'
  + link back to /data/[format]
- No snapshot data: header shows archetype info, charts show 'No data yet' state
- No recent results: 'No results recorded yet' — not an error
- Win rate empty: pending state (not error) — matches table is new

---

SEO
- <title>: '[Archetype Name] — [Format] Metagame — Firemind'
- <meta description>: '[Archetype Name] is a [tier] deck in [format] with [meta_share]% meta share. See recent results, trends, and oracle analysis.'
- OG image: archetype name, format, meta%, trend arrow (toy.11)
- All content real HTML — crawlable

---

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Correct archetype data loads for known IDs, 404 for unknown
- [ ] ArchetypeHeader shows all available stats, win rate shows pending state when matches empty
- [ ] ShareOverTime handles < 3 data points gracefully (shows dots + note)

description:
V0 rate limiting — ships before Lemon Squeezy. Unblocks toy.8 (/chat full UI) without waiting for the full tier system. Depends only on toy.1 (Google OAuth — already closed).

This bead intentionally defers tier distinction (Casual vs Spike), onboarding bonuses, upgrade CTAs, and server-side anonymous enforcement to toy.7, which ships after Lemon Squeezy (toy.2) is set up. toy.7 will replace/upgrade the logic built here.

---

## LIMITS (V0 — NO TIERS)

- Anonymous: 5 queries per browser-day. Tracked in localStorage. Client-enforces before calling API.
- Logged in (any authenticated user): 10 queries per day. Tracked server-side in oracle_queries table. Reset at midnight UTC.
- No distinction between Casual and Spike yet — all logged-in users are equal for now.
- No onboarding bonus, no upgrade CTA (nowhere to upgrade to — just show the countdown).

---

## DB SCHEMA

New table (migration required):
  CREATE TABLE oracle_queries (
    user_id   uuid    NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users ON DELETE CASCADE,
    date      date    NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
    count     integer NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, date)
  );

No changes to the users table needed for v0 (signup_date, tier columns etc. are deferred to toy.7).

---

## API ROUTE — /api/query CHANGES

Add rate limit check at the top of the route handler, before calling the LLM:

  1. Try to get authenticated user from Supabase session (cookie).
  2. If authenticated:
       a. SELECT count FROM oracle_queries WHERE user_id = $1 AND date = CURRENT_DATE
       b. If count >= 10: return 429 with rate_limit payload (see below)
       c. If under limit: continue, then upsert after successful response:
            INSERT INTO oracle_queries (user_id, date, count) VALUES ($1, CURRENT_DATE, 1)
            ON CONFLICT (user_id, date) DO UPDATE SET count = oracle_queries.count + 1
  3. If anonymous: no server-side check — client gate handles this (localStorage).

Rate limit state in every successful response (add to existing response shape):
  {
    ...existing fields...,
    rate_limit: {
      remaining: number,        // 10 - new_count (authenticated) or null (anonymous)
      resets_at: string,        // ISO8601 midnight UTC tonight, e.g. '2026-03-05T00:00:00.000Z'
      tier: 'anon' | 'user'     // v0 only knows two states; toy.7 adds 'casual' | 'spike'
    }
  }

On limit exceeded:
  HTTP 429: { error: 'rate_limit_exceeded', rate_limit: { remaining: 0, resets_at: string, tier: 'user' } }

resets_at calculation (reusable helper):
  const now = new Date()
  const midnight = new Date(Date.UTC(now.getUTCFullYear(), now.getUTCMonth(), now.getUTCDate() + 1))
  return midnight.toISOString()

---

## CLIENT-SIDE — ANONYMOUS GATE (localStorage)

localStorage key: 'fm_anon_queries'
Schema: { count: number, date: 'YYYY-MM-DD' }

On every query attempt (before calling /api/query):
  1. Read fm_anon_queries from localStorage.
  2. If date !== today (UTC): reset to { count: 0, date: today }.
  3. If count >= 5: do NOT call API — show inline auth prompt instead (see below).
  4. If count < 5: call API, on response increment count, write back to localStorage.

Date comparison must use UTC date (not local date) to match server reset at midnight UTC:
  const todayUTC = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10)

---

## CHAT UI — ANONYMOUS STATE

### Progressive counter (appears in response meta footer, same line as confidence/window_days)
- Before query 1 / empty state: no counter. Clean slate.
- After query 1 response: '4 free queries remaining' — text-xs text-ash. Low prominence.
- After query 2 response: '3 free queries remaining' — text-xs text-ash.
- After query 3 response: '2 free queries remaining' — text-xs text-gold. Gentle warning.
- After query 4 response: '1 free query remaining' — text-xs text-flame.
- After query 5 response: inline auth prompt card appears (see below). Input disabled.

### Input bar at anonymous limit
- Input: visible but disabled (opacity-50)
- Placeholder changes to: 'Sign in to keep going…'
- No counter above input — the auth card in the conversation is primary

### Inline auth prompt card
Position: in the conversation flow, below the 5th response. Left-aligned, same width as oracle messages.
Styling: copper/flame gradient left border — distinct from oracle's spark-blue oracle-border.
  border-left: 2px solid (gradient from copper #B87333 to flame #D4552A top-to-bottom)
  Closest Tailwind: custom inline style or a new utility class

Content:
  ┌─ copper-flame left border ──────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                          │
  │  You've got good questions.                              │
  │  Create a free account to keep going.                    │
  │  No credit card, ever.                                   │
  │                                                          │
  │  [G  Sign in with Google]  ← full-width primary button  │
  │                                                          │
  │  or wait · resets in 09:14:33  ← live HH:MM:SS ticker  │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

- No dismiss button. Prompt is persistent.
- 'or wait · resets in HH:MM:SS': live countdown to midnight UTC. text-xs text-ash.
  Same urgency mechanic as the logged-in countdown — makes signing up feel easier than waiting.
- Input placeholder: 'Sign in to keep going…'

### OAuth popup — context preservation
Use Supabase signInWithOAuth({ provider: 'google', options: { skipBrowserRedirect: false } })
with a popup flow (not a full redirect) so conversation state in React is preserved.
On popup success, onAuthStateChange fires in main window → remove auth card → re-enable input → user continues.
Popup blocked fallback: serialize messages to localStorage before redirect, restore on return to /chat.

---

## CHAT UI — LOGGED-IN STATE (all authenticated users, no tier distinction)

### Query counter — always visible above input bar, right-aligned
States:
  ≥ 3 remaining:  'X queries left today'        text-xs text-ash   (low prominence)
  2 remaining:    '2 queries left today'         text-xs text-gold  (amber warning)
  1 remaining:    '1 query left today'           text-xs text-flame (red warning)
  0 remaining:    counter replaced with HH:MM:SS countdown (see below)

### Input bar at logged-in limit
- Input: visible but disabled (opacity-50)
- Counter replaced with live countdown:
    '⏱ Resets in 03:42:17'
    ⏱ in text-ash, HH:MM:SS in text-ink monospace tabular-nums, updating every second
- NO upgrade CTA for v0 — nowhere to upgrade to yet. Plain countdown only.
  (toy.7 adds 'Go Spike →' CTA alongside the countdown when tiers exist)

---

## WHAT THIS BEAD INTENTIONALLY OMITS (deferred to toy.7)

- Casual vs Spike tier distinction
- 7-day onboarding bonus (15 queries/day for new signups)
- 'Go Spike →' upgrade CTA at limit
- server-side enforcement for anonymous users (too complex without a user_id)
- signup_date column on users table
- chat history gating
- feature gating middleware (toy.3)

---

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] oracle_queries table created via migration
- [ ] /api/query returns rate_limit object on every successful response
- [ ] /api/query returns 429 with rate_limit when authenticated user hits 10/day
- [ ] Unauthenticated requests are not blocked server-side (client gate only)
- [ ] localStorage gate prevents anonymous users calling API after 5 queries in a day
- [ ] Anonymous UTC date comparison resets correctly at midnight UTC
- [ ] Progressive counter appears in response meta footer at correct thresholds (text-ash / gold / flame)
- [ ] Inline auth prompt appears after 5th anonymous response with correct styling and live countdown
- [ ] [Sign in with Google] opens OAuth popup (not redirect), conversation preserved on auth success
- [ ] Logged-in counter visible throughout session with correct color thresholds
- [ ] Logged-in countdown is live HH:MM:SS, no upgrade CTA
- [ ] All states tested: fresh anon, anon at limit, logged-in under limit, logged-in at limit
title: V0 rate limiting — anonymous 5 queries, logged-in 10/day, no tiers

description:
Two optimizations to reduce query latency:

## 1. Use Haiku for intent extraction

extractIntent() currently uses claude-sonnet-4-6 for a simple structured JSON extraction (format, question_type, archetype, card, timeframe_days). This is overkill — Haiku can do this in ~300-500ms vs Sonnet's ~1.5-2s.

- Add model parameter to LLMProvider.complete() and completeWithHistory()
- extractIntent() passes model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'
- Final answer generation stays on Sonnet

## 2. Stream the final response

The final LLM call is 70-80% of total query time (8-10s). Streaming delivers first token in ~1s — users read while it generates.

Three layers need changes:

### LLM Provider (src/lib/llm.ts)
- Add completeStream() method to LLMProvider interface
- Returns AsyncIterable of text deltas
- Uses Anthropic SDK stream() method

### API Route (src/app/api/query/route.ts)
- Switch from JSON response to SSE/ReadableStream for the answer portion
- Intent extraction + retrieval still happen before stream starts
- Send metadata (intent, data, rate_limit) as first SSE event
- Stream answer tokens as subsequent events
- Send done event at end

### Chat Page (src/app/chat/page.tsx)
- Replace fetch+JSON with EventSource or fetch+ReadableStream reader
- Render oracle response incrementally as tokens arrive
- Keep thinking animation until first token
- Meta footer appears after stream completes

## Acceptance criteria
- extractIntent uses Haiku, final answer uses Sonnet
- Trace logs confirm extractIntent dropped from ~1.5-2s to ~300-500ms
- API streams the final answer via SSE
- Chat UI renders tokens incrementally
- Thinking animation shows until first token arrives
- Meta footer + rate limit counter appear after stream completes
- Rate limit upsert happens after stream completes (not before)
- Cache still works (stores completed response)
- All existing tests pass (mock streaming in tests)
- npx tsc --noEmit clean
title: Optimize LLM call performance — Haiku for intent + streaming final response

description:
Lemon Squeezy subscription integration. Lemon Squeezy is the merchant of record — they handle all VAT, global sales tax, and year-end tax reporting. You never touch tax infrastructure.

Supabase is the fast-path source of truth for tier; Lemon Squeezy webhooks keep it in sync. Never query Lemon Squeezy at request time — read users.tier from Supabase.

---

WHY LEMON SQUEEZY OVER STRIPE
Lemon Squeezy is merchant of record: they collect and remit VAT/GST globally, handle US sales tax by state, and issue a single 1099 at year end. Stripe leaves all of that to you. For a solo indie product, this is the correct trade.

---

DATA MODEL ADDITIONS (migration required):
  lemon_customer_id         text unique  -- null until first checkout
  lemon_subscription_id     text unique  -- null until active subscription
  lemon_subscription_status text         -- 'active' | 'past_due' | 'cancelled' | 'expired' | null
  spike_started_at          timestamptz  -- set on first upgrade, never cleared

signup_date date should already exist from toy.1. Confirm before migrating.

---

LEMON SQUEEZY PRODUCTS

Two variants on one product (Firemind Spike):
  LS_VARIANT_MONTHLY  ($4.99/month)
  LS_VARIANT_ANNUAL   ($47.88/year ~$3.99/mo, 20% off)
Create in LS dashboard, store variant IDs as env vars.

---

API ROUTES

POST /api/ls/checkout
- Auth required (anonymous gets 401 with error: 'create_account_first')
- Body: { variant: 'monthly' | 'annual' }
- Use @lemonsqueezy/lemonsqueezy.js SDK
- createCheckout(storeId, variantId, { checkoutData: { email, custom: { supabase_user_id } }, productOptions: { redirectUrl: /settings?upgrade=success } })
- Return { url: checkout.data.attributes.url }
- Client redirects to LS hosted checkout — never build a custom card form

POST /api/ls/portal
- Auth required + lemon_subscription_id
- Redirect to LS customer portal (handles cancel, payment method, invoices)

POST /api/webhooks/lemonsqueezy
- Verify HMAC-SHA256 signature: X-Signature header vs createHmac('sha256', LS_WEBHOOK_SECRET).update(rawBody).digest('hex')
- Raw body required — disable body parser on this route
- Event name in body.meta.event_name

  subscription_created:
    users SET tier='spike', lemon_subscription_id, lemon_customer_id, lemon_subscription_status='active', spike_started_at=now()
    WHERE id = body.meta.custom_data.supabase_user_id

  subscription_updated:
    users SET lemon_subscription_status=status WHERE lemon_subscription_id=body.data.id
    if active: also SET tier='spike'
    if cancelled or expired: also SET tier='casual'

  subscription_cancelled:
    users SET lemon_subscription_status='cancelled' (keep tier='spike' until period end — LS fires subscription_expired at actual expiry)

  subscription_expired:
    users SET tier='casual', lemon_subscription_status='expired', lemon_subscription_id=null

  subscription_payment_failed:
    users SET lemon_subscription_status='past_due' (keep tier='spike' — grace period, LS retries)

  subscription_payment_success:
    users SET tier='spike', lemon_subscription_status='active'

  Always return 200 for unhandled event types.

---

GRACE PERIOD
LS retries failed payments. During retries status='past_due', tier stays 'spike'. Drops to casual only on subscription_expired. Never punish users for temporary card issues.

---

POST-CHECKOUT UX
/settings?upgrade=success:
1. Show 'Activating your Spike subscription...' + spinner
2. Supabase Realtime subscription on users row for current user_id
3. On tier flip to 'spike': show 'You are now on Spike.' success state
4. Timeout at 10s: 'Taking longer than expected — refresh the page'
Do NOT poll. Realtime only.

---

ANONYMOUS + GO SPIKE
Must have account first. Flow: Go Spike -> if anonymous -> inline auth prompt -> after OAuth -> /pricing -> checkout.

---

ENV VARS
  LEMONSQUEEZY_API_KEY
  LS_WEBHOOK_SECRET
  LS_STORE_ID
  LS_VARIANT_MONTHLY
  LS_VARIANT_ANNUAL
notes: GOTCHA — cancellation vs expiry are two separate events: When a user cancels, LS fires subscription_cancelled immediately but the subscription runs to the end of the billing period. The actual access downgrade happens when subscription_expired fires (could be weeks later). If the engineer only handles subscription_cancelled and sets tier='casual' there, users get cut off the moment they cancel instead of at period end. The correct pattern: subscription_cancelled -> status='cancelled', tier stays 'spike'. subscription_expired -> status='expired', tier='casual'. Both events must be handled.
title: Lemon Squeezy subscription integration — Casual/Spike tiers

description:
Server-side feature gating. Reads users.tier from Supabase — never queries Lemon Squeezy at request time. Lemon Squeezy webhooks (toy.2) keep users.tier current.

---

TIER RESOLUTION

Helper: getUserTier(supabase, userId) -> 'casual' | 'spike'
  const { data } = await supabase.from('users').select('tier, signup_date').eq('id', userId).single()
  return data?.tier ?? 'casual'

For oracle query counting, also need signup_date to compute onboarding bonus window.

---

QUERY LIMITS
- Anonymous: 3/day — client-side localStorage, no DB. API trusts client. (Enforced in toy.7)
- Casual: 15/day for first 7 days post-signup, then 5/day. Tracked in oracle_queries table.
- Spike: 30/day.

---

CHAT HISTORY
- Anonymous: ephemeral, session only — no DB storage
- Casual: read-only access to last 7 days of conversations. Can view but not resume/continue threads. History drawer shows with 'Upgrade to resume past conversations' inline CTA.
- Spike: unlimited history, fully resumable and searchable

---

OTHER FEATURE GATES
All gates are soft inline — no hard redirects. Gated features render with an upgrade CTA in place of the feature itself.
- Deck list generation: Spike only. Casual sees: 'Unlock deck generation with Spike — $4.99/mo [Upgrade]'
- Matchup matrix on archetype pages: Spike only. Same inline CTA pattern.
- Alert subscriptions: 1 archetype for Casual, unlimited for Spike. Casual at limit sees: 'Upgrade for unlimited alerts'
- Email alerts + weekly digest: Spike only. Casual sees features described with upgrade prompt.
- Chat history resume: Spike only (Casual has read-only view).

---

GATING MIDDLEWARE PATTERN
Applied at the API route level (not just client-side):
  1. Get userId from Supabase JWT
  2. getUserTier() — single DB read
  3. Check feature against tier
  4. Return 403 with { error: 'upgrade_required', feature: string } if blocked
Client renders inline upgrade prompts based on 403 responses or pre-fetched tier state. Never blank — always shows the CTA.

---

SUBSCRIPTION STATUS EDGE CASES
past_due: treat as Spike (grace period — user keeps access while Lemon Squeezy retries payment)
cancelled: keep as Spike until subscription_expired fires (LS cancels at period end by default)
expired: treat as Casual (webhook has already updated tier)
null/missing: treat as Casual (safe default)

---

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] getUserTier() is the single shared helper — no scattered tier checks
- [ ] All feature gates enforced server-side, not client-only
- [ ] past_due and cancelled treated as Spike (grace period)
- [ ] Inline upgrade CTAs render correctly for all gated features
- [ ] No hard redirects anywhere
title: Feature gating middleware — enforce tier limits

Typography: Geist/Space Grotesk for display, Geist Mono for data. Blueprint schematic background texture (very low opacity). Electric-blue glow on interactive borders. All secondary views (history, alerts, archetype detail) open as drawers over the current page. See product/information-architecture.md.

description:
Backend rate limiting for oracle queries at /api/query.

## Limit rules
- Anonymous: 3 queries/day. Tracked client-side in localStorage as { count: number, date: 'YYYY-MM-DD' }. Client enforces before hitting API; API trusts client for anonymous (no user_id). Reset at midnight UTC — date comparison against today.
- Casual: 15/day for first 7 days post-signup (signup_date stored on users row), then 5/day. Tracked server-side in oracle_queries table: { user_id, date, count }. Upsert on each query.
- Spike: 30/day. Same tracking.

## API response shape
Every successful query response includes rate limit state:
  {
    answer: string,
    ...existing fields...,
    rate_limit: {
      remaining: number,
      resets_at: string,  // ISO8601, always midnight UTC of current day
      tier: 'anon' | 'casual' | 'spike'
    }
  }

On limit exceeded, return 429 with:
  { error: 'rate_limit_exceeded', rate_limit: { remaining: 0, resets_at: string, tier } }

## Anonymous query pool is global per-browser-day
The landing page input at / routes to /chat?q=... which auto-submits on /chat. All oracle queries go through /chat → /api/query, so the anonymous count is naturally unified. No cross-page coordination needed.

## DB schema addition
oracle_queries table:
  user_id uuid references auth.users
  date date
  count integer default 1
  PRIMARY KEY (user_id, date)

users table addition:
  signup_date date not null default current_date  (set on first OAuth login upsert)

## Acceptance
Anonymous: 3/day via localStorage, 429 on 4th, resets_at in response. Casual: correct tier-aware limit with 7-day bonus. Spike: 30/day. All 429s include resets_at.
title: Oracle query rate limiting — server-side enforcement

description:
Full /chat UI. Three user states: anonymous, casual, spike. Rate limit state drives most of the conditional UI.

---

## PHILOSOPHY
The rate limit is a conversion funnel, not a punishment. Every state has a clear next action. The oracle response always renders completely before any limit prompt — value first, ask second. All prompts are inline in the conversation. No modals. No redirects. No dead ends.

---

## ANONYMOUS STATE (no account)

### Query counter — progressive disclosure
- Before query 1 / empty state: NO counter visible. Clean slate, no anxiety.
- After query 1 response renders: subtle 'X of 3 free queries used' appears in the meta footer area of that response (same text-xs text-ash style as confidence/window_days). Low prominence. Informational only.
- After query 2 response renders: '2 of 3 free queries used' in same position, but text-gold. The query input still works normally.
- After query 3 response renders: input is disabled. The inline auth prompt card appears in the conversation flow below the response (see below).

### Input bar — anonymous at limit
- Input field: visible but disabled (opacity-50)
- No counter text above input — the inline auth card in the conversation is the primary communication
- Input placeholder changes to: 'Sign in to keep going…'

### Inline auth prompt card (appears after 3rd response)
This is the most important UI element in the anonymous flow. It must feel like a companion message, not a paywall.

Styling: same visual position as an oracle message (left-aligned, full width of conversation). NOT the spark-blue oracle-border. Use a copper/flame gradient left border instead — visually distinct from oracle responses, still in the design language.

  border-left: 2px solid; 
  border-image: linear-gradient(to bottom, #B87333, #D4552A) 1;
  (or closest Tailwind equivalent using copper/flame tokens)

Content:
  ┌─ copper-flame left border ──────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                          │
  │  You've got good questions.                              │
  │  Create a free account to keep going — no credit        │
  │  card, ever.                                             │
  │                                                          │
  │  [G  Sign in with Google]   (primary button, full width) │
  │                                                          │
  │  or wait · resets in 09:14:33   (text-xs text-ash,      │
  │                                  live HH:MM:SS ticker)  │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

- NO dismiss button. The prompt is persistent. Scroll up and the input is still disabled.
- 'You've got good questions.' — affirmative framing. The oracle is impressed.
- 'or wait · resets in HH:MM:SS' — live countdown, same midnight UTC anchor. Gives users who resist signup a real alternative. Builds goodwill. Also makes signup feel easier by comparison.

### OAuth flow — context preservation
Use Supabase signInWithOAuth with popup: true. The main window keeps React state (conversation) alive.
On popup auth success, onAuthStateChange fires in the main window → upgrade session → remove auth card → re-enable input → user continues exactly where they left off.
Popup blocked fallback: serialize messages to localStorage before redirect, restore on return to /chat.

---

## CASUAL STATE (free account, authenticated)

### Query counter — always visible, color-coded
Lives above the input bar, right-aligned. Shows throughout the session.

States:
- ≥3 remaining: 'X queries left today'  →  text-xs text-ash  (low prominence)
- 2 remaining:  '2 queries left today'  →  text-xs text-gold  (amber warning)
- 1 remaining:  '1 query left today · Go Spike →'  →  text-xs text-flame  (CTA appears early, at 1 remaining, before they hit the wall)
- 0 remaining:  counter replaced with live countdown (see below)

### Input bar — Casual at limit
- Input: visible but disabled (opacity-50)
- Above input (where counter was): '⏱ Resets in 03:42:17 · Go Spike →'
  - ⏱ in text-ash
  - HH:MM:SS in text-ink, monospace font (tabular-nums), live ticker updating every second
  - ' · ' separator
  - 'Go Spike →' in text-spark, hover:underline — links to /pricing or opens upgrade modal

The ticking clock is the conversion mechanic. It makes the alternative (waiting 3+ hours) concrete and slightly tedious without being hostile. Users mid-research session who hit the wall will convert here.

### No inline card
Casual limit is communicated entirely via the counter/countdown in the input bar. No conversation-interrupting card. They're authenticated, they know the rules, the counter has been counting down all session.

---

## SPIKE STATE (paid)

### Query counter
'X / 30 today' — text-xs text-ash, right-aligned above input. Informational only.
Never disabled. Never countdown. Never a CTA. Just a number.

---

## EMPTY STATE (no messages yet, all tiers)
Centered suggested prompts with radial-gradient glow (already implemented).
Counter for Casual/Spike shows above input.
Anonymous: no counter on empty state.

---

## ONBOARDING BONUS (Casual, days 1-7)
Counter shows '15 queries left today' during bonus period. No special UI callout — just the higher number. On day 8 when it drops to 5, no notification — they'll see '5 queries left today' on first load.

---

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] All three tiers render correct counter states
- [ ] Anonymous counter appears only after query 1 (not before)
- [ ] Inline auth card appears after query 3 response with copper-flame border, correct copy, live countdown
- [ ] OAuth popup preserves conversation context
- [ ] Casual countdown is live HH:MM:SS updating every second
- [ ] 'Go Spike →' CTA appears at 1 remaining (Casual) and at limit (Casual)
- [ ] Spike sees no warning states, no countdown
- [ ] Input disabled correctly at limit for both anonymous and casual
title: /chat — full chat UI

description:
Public SSR page, no auth required. Crawlable. Data freshness: revalidate every 3600s (ISR) — scraper runs every 12h so 1hr cache is a good balance of performance vs staleness.

URL: /data/[format] where format = 'modern' | 'standard'
URL params: ?range=7d|30d|90d (default: 30d). Range drives which snapshot windows are included. Range is a URL param (not client state) so pages are shareable and crawlable at each range.

/data alone redirects to /data/modern.

---

DATA QUERIES (run in Server Component, direct Supabase calls — no API route needed for SSR pages)

1. Meta share + top movers:
   SELECT
     ms.meta_share, ms.trend_delta, ms.top8_count, ms.total_entries,
     ms.sample_size, ms.confidence, ms.window_end,
     a.id, a.name, a.tier
   FROM metagame_snapshots ms
   JOIN archetypes a ON a.id = ms.archetype_id
   WHERE ms.format = [format]
     AND ms.window_end >= now() - interval '[range]'
   ORDER BY ms.window_end DESC, ms.meta_share DESC

   Take the most recent window_end as "current snapshot". Use trend_delta for movers.

2. Trend lines (multi-window history):
   Same query without the window_end filter — pull all snapshots for the format,
   grouped by archetype. Take only top 8 archetypes by current meta_share to keep
   the chart readable. Order by window_end ascending for chart x-axis.

3. Format header stats (total decks analyzed, date range, confidence):
   Derived from the snapshot rows — max(sample_size), max(confidence), min/max(window_end).

All three queries run in parallel via Promise.all.

---

COMPONENTS

MetaShareBar:
- Recharts HorizontalBarChart (BarChart with layout='vertical')
- Y axis: archetype name (links to /data/[format]/[archetype_id])
- X axis: meta_share %
- Bar fill: spark (#4F8EF7) at 70% opacity, full opacity on hover
- Tier badge (S/A/B/C) left of name, color-coded: S=spark, A=gold, B=copper, C=ash
- Sort toggle (client-side only, no refetch): sort by meta share (default) or win rate
  Win rate sort: disabled with tooltip 'Win rate requires match data' until matches table has rows
- Time range tabs: 7d / 30d / 90d — these are links that change ?range= param, not client state

TopMoversWidget:
- NOT a chart — a two-column ranked list: Gainers | Losers
- Derived from trend_delta on the current snapshot
- Show top 3 gainers (trend_delta > 0, sorted desc) and top 3 losers (trend_delta < 0, sorted asc)
- Format: 'Boros Energy +3.8%' in text-spark, 'Rhinos -2.1%' in text-flame
- Empty state: 'Trend data requires multiple snapshot windows — check back soon'
  (shown when all trend_delta values are null or 0, which is the case with a single snapshot window)

TrendLines:
- Recharts LineChart
- X axis: window_end dates
- Y axis: meta_share %
- One Line per top 8 archetype, each a different color (spark palette with opacity variants)
- Hoverable legend, archetype names link to detail pages
- Minimum viable: need >= 3 data points per archetype for a meaningful line
  Empty state: 'Not enough history yet — trend lines appear after multiple weeks of data'

ConfidenceBadge:
- Reuse the same LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH/VERY HIGH treatment from the oracle (text-flame/gold/spark)
- Shown in the page header: 'Based on [sample_size] decks · [confidence]'
- Tooltip: 'Confidence reflects sample size and recency of tournament data'

OracleSummaryCard (bottom of page):
- Static component — no oracle query on page load (too expensive for SSR)
- Shows: '⚡ Ask the Firemind about [format]' with a pre-filled query link to /chat?q=...
- Example: '?q=What%27s+dominating+Modern+right+now%3F'
- CTA button links to /chat — uses a query quota when clicked

---

LOADING STATES (Suspense boundaries per section)

Each section (MetaShareBar, TopMovers, TrendLines) is wrapped in its own Suspense with a skeleton:
- MetaShareBar skeleton: 8 gray rounded bars of varying width, staggered opacity
- TopMovers skeleton: 6 gray text lines in two columns
- TrendLines skeleton: a flat gray area where the chart will be

Use Tailwind animate-pulse on all skeleton elements.
Do NOT show a full-page spinner — sections load independently.

---

EMPTY / NO DATA STATES

If metagame_snapshots has no rows for this format:
  Show full-page empty state: 'No data yet for [format] — check back after the next scrape runs'
  with a timestamp of when the next scrape is expected (hardcode '12 hours' for now)

If snapshots exist but trend_delta is all null (single window):
  TopMovers: 'Trend data appears after multiple events — check back soon'
  TrendLines: 'Not enough history yet'
  MetaShareBar: renders normally (single window is enough for a bar chart)

---

PAGE HEADER
Format name + format selector toggle (Modern / Standard tabs)
'Last updated: [computed_at of most recent snapshot]'
ConfidenceBadge

---

SEO
- <title>: 'Modern Metagame — Firemind' (server-rendered)
- <meta description>: 'Top Modern decks this week: [top 3 archetype names] leading the field.'
- OG image: format name + top 3 archetypes + meta share % (toy.11)
- All archetype names in the bar chart are real HTML text — crawlable

---

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] /data redirects to /data/modern
- [ ] ?range param drives snapshot window, default 30d, all three values work
- [ ] MetaShareBar renders with real data, archetype names link to detail pages
- [ ] TopMovers shows empty state gracefully when trend_delta is null
- [ ] TrendLines shows empty state gracefully when < 3 data points
- [ ] Each section has independent Suspense skeleton
- [ ] ConfidenceBadge matches oracle visual treatment
- [ ] Page is fully SSR, revalidates every hour
- [ ] No auth required, no client-side gating
notes: PREREQUISITE: metagame_snapshots must be populated by the analyzer before this page has anything meaningful to show. If the table is empty, the format overview hits its full-page empty state immediately. Check SELECT COUNT(*) FROM metagame_snapshots WHERE format = 'modern' before starting UI work — if zero, the analyzer needs to run first or the page will ship with nothing but empty states.

description: mtggoldfish.ts and mtgtop8.ts call resolveCardIds() inside the per-deck loop — one DB query per deck. mtgo.ts correctly batches all card names upfront before the loop. Fix both goldfish and top8 to collect all card names across all decks first, then do a single resolveCardIds call, and pass the pre-built map into each deck's row construction.
title: Parser N+1: batch card ID resolution in goldfish and top8 parsers

description: Cached queries return instantly which feels jarring — the response appears before the user has even finished processing that they hit submit. Add a minimum 1-second delay (e.g. Promise.all with a sleep) so the OracleThinking animation has time to display. Only apply to fast responses; if the query already takes >1s, no added delay.
title: Add 1-second artificial delay to cached oracle queries

description: fetchHtml() in src/scrapers/mtgo.ts has no timeout. If mtgo.com hangs, the cron/worker hangs until Render kills the process. Add AbortController with 30s timeout.
title: Add fetch timeout to MTGO scraper

description: parsePending* functions in all parsers do: SELECT WHERE status='pending', then process, then UPDATE status='parsed'. If two workers run simultaneously both see the same pending jobs and both process them. sync_deck_cards RPC is transactional so deck_cards won't corrupt, but scrape_job status ends up as 'parsed' from whichever finishes last. Not a current problem (single cron) but will break immediately if parallelized. Fix: UPDATE status='in_progress' WHERE status='pending' RETURNING id,raw_content as the claim step, then only process claimed rows.
title: parsers: scrape_jobs status update is not an atomic claim — SELECT pending + UPDATE parsed is a TOCTOU race if workers ever parallelize

description: In retrieveContext, sum (quantity * card.usd) and (quantity * card.tix) across mainboard cards for each deck in top_decks. Add deck_cost_usd and deck_cost_tix to DeckSummary. Include in assembleContext output. Update RESPONSE_SYSTEM to instruct LLM to rank by price when user asks about affordability/budget. Example query: 'most affordable Modern deck that wins consistently'.
title: Compute deck cost in retrieval layer and surface in query responses

description: In retrieval.ts, three .in() calls are unbounded: allNames in attachDeckCosts (24 decks * unique card names), tourneyIds in fetchCardInfo, and deckIds in fetchCardInfo. At scale these exceed PostgREST URL limits causing silent empty/partial results. Fix: chunk allNames at 100 items in attachDeckCosts; rewrite fetchCardInfo to avoid .in() chaining via a direct join query or RPC.
title: retrieval: chunk unbounded .in() arrays — tourneyIds/deckIds/allNames can exceed PostgREST 8KB URL limit causing silent partial results
Implement in-memory query cache with 1hr TTL and 200-entry cap
